notes: Stage 3 Linux boot directed debug probes. These rows are functional RTL diagnostics, not performance signoff rows.

benchmarks.name: rvc_addi16sp_712d_smoke
benchmarks.notes: Directed check for Linux entry instruction 0x712d, C.ADDI16SP, which must expand to addi sp, sp, -288.

benchmarks.name: probe_linux_beq_after_call
benchmarks.notes: Mirrors the Linux OF lookup shape where a call result in a0 is compared against s3 by a following BEQ.

benchmarks.name: probe_dcache_p0_miss_retry
benchmarks.notes: Directed LSU/D-cache probe for the Linux mtimer deadlock class: independent cold loads pressure D-cache MSHR allocation and require load port 0 retry to preserve endpoint identity.

benchmarks.name: probe_lq_full_load_completion
benchmarks.notes: Directed LQ/ROB pressure probe for the current Linux mtimer freeze class: many cold loads target x0 so every ROB load must complete without consuming physical integer registers.

benchmarks.name: probe_load_spec_cancel_partial_flush
benchmarks.notes: Directed LSU/IQ probe for the Stage 3 Linux NULL-deref class: an older cold load and dependent branch must not keep a stale speculative-ready bit when a younger branch causes a partial flush.

benchmarks.name: probe_mtimer_head_load_pressure
benchmarks.notes: Directed M-mode timer-handler-body probe for the Linux mtimer_event_start freeze: a cold handler head load must complete while younger independent cacheable loads create ROB/LQ pressure. It runs the body directly because tb_top does not model CLINT timer interrupts.

benchmarks.name: probe_mtimer_head_load_lq_full
benchmarks.notes: Stronger mtimer_event_start probe for the current Linux no-retire class: the handler head load must complete while younger cold loads fill and drain the LQ/LMB path.
